articles.title: QBIX Play Immersive Gaming Room Expands Globally for FEC Operators
articles.summary: Inowize is celebrating worldwide adoption of QBIX Play, a 6-player immersive gaming room designed specifically for FECs, wristband venues, and party facilities. The system fits in 169 square feet, requires no dedicated staff, and features nine themed titles with sessions under five minutes, making it a low-overhead, high-throughput attraction. A limited summer demo offer is available for operators considering adding the experience.

articles.title: How Trade Tariffs Are Driving Up Attraction Costs for US Amusement Operators
articles.summary: A detailed analysis reveals that US trade tariffs on imported amusement equipment are adding significant costs to new attraction projects, sometimes nearly doubling total budgets when combined with installation, shipping, and theming expenses. FEC and amusement park operators planning capital investments in rides or dark rides from European or Asian manufacturers need to factor tariff exposure into their financial modeling. The piece is a timely reminder to revisit supplier contracts and sourcing strategies before committing to new build-outs.

articles.title: Intercard Cashless Technology Selected for New Sports-Anchored FEC in Illinois
articles.summary: Intercard has been chosen to power the arcade at Legends Entertainment inside Scheels Sports Park in Springfield, Illinois, a 46,000-square-foot facility targeting sports tournament visitors and their families. The venue sits within the Legacy Pointe mixed-use development, illustrating the growing trend of pairing competitive youth sports facilities with adjacent FEC entertainment. This deployment highlights cashless card systems as a key operational backbone for high-volume, tournament-driven entertainment venues.

articles.title: Universal Kids Resort Opens in Frisco, Texas with Seven Themed Areas
articles.summary: Universal Kids Resort has officially opened in Frisco, Texas, featuring seven dedicated zones built around beloved children's characters and stories, three years after the project was announced. The opening represents a major new family entertainment destination in the Dallas-Fort Worth market and will increase competitive pressure on regional FECs and indoor entertainment venues in the area. Operators in the region should monitor attendance patterns and adjust programming or marketing strategies accordingly.

articles.title: Vulcania Amusement Park Unifies Ticketing, POS, and Access Control on Single Vintia Platform
articles.summary: French amusement park Vulcania, which welcomes roughly 400,000 visitors annually, is adopting Vintia's Recreatex and Enviso platform to consolidate ticketing, booking, point-of-sale, and access control into one integrated system over the next four years. The move reflects a broader industry shift toward unified operations platforms that reduce friction for guests and administrative burden for staff. FEC operators evaluating technology upgrades can look to this deployment as a real-world case study in large-scale platform integration.

articles.title: Five Iron Golf Launches in London as Part of £20M UK Expansion
articles.summary: US indoor golf brand Five Iron Golf has opened its first London location as part of a £20 million UK expansion plan, signaling strong international appetite for upscale indoor sports entertainment concepts. The move underscores how tech-enhanced, social golf formats continue to attract significant investment and expand into new markets beyond North America. FEC operators and investors should take note of the format's scalability and the consumer demand driving its growth.

articles.title: LEVEL 99 Adventure Entertainment Venue to Open First Connecticut Location in West Hartford
articles.summary: LEVEL 99, the activity-based entertainment concept featuring dozens of physical skill challenges, is expanding into Connecticut with a new West Hartford location. The brand's continued geographic growth demonstrates sustained consumer demand for non-traditional, active entertainment experiences that appeal to teens and young adults. Regional FEC operators should be aware of LEVEL 99's expanding footprint as it enters new competitive markets.

articles.title: Jake's Unlimited FEC Finds New Direction Under Local Ownership
articles.summary: Jake's Unlimited, a large-format family entertainment center in Arizona, is charting a new course after being acquired by a local owner following its previous corporate ownership. The transition highlights challenges and opportunities that arise when major FEC brands change hands, including the potential to reconnect with community roots and rebuild guest loyalty. The story offers useful perspective for operators and investors considering FEC acquisitions or turnaround situations.

articles.title: The Breakers Resort Plans New Family Entertainment Center in Palm Beach
articles.summary: Renderings have been released for a planned Family Entertainment Center at The Breakers, the iconic Palm Beach luxury resort, signaling that upscale hospitality properties are increasingly integrating FEC concepts into their amenity mix. The development reflects a broader trend of hotels and resorts adding family-focused entertainment to drive ancillary revenue and extend guest stays. FEC designers and operators may find opportunities in the hospitality sector as this trend grows.

articles.title: New Plans Revealed for West Des Moines Waterpark and Entertainment Complex
articles.summary: Updated plans have been released for a waterpark and entertainment complex in West Des Moines, Iowa, pointing to continued investment in large-scale family leisure destinations in the Midwest. Projects of this scale often reshape regional entertainment markets and create both competitive pressure and supply chain opportunities for local FEC operators and vendors. Stakeholders in the Iowa and broader Midwest market should track this development as it moves through approvals.

articles.title: Sally Dark Rides Debuts Kong x Godzilla: The Ride at Lotte World
articles.summary: Sally Dark Rides has collaborated with Legendary Entertainment and Toho Co. to open Kong x Godzilla: The Ride at Lotte World Adventure in South Korea, marking Legendary's first-ever theme park attraction. The multimedia dark ride showcases the continued demand for IP-driven immersive experiences and positions Sally Dark Rides as a key partner for major entertainment properties seeking theme park presence. Suppliers and FEC developers looking at dark ride technology or IP licensing deals should follow this launch closely.

articles.title: Altitude Trampoline Park to Open New Location in Litchfield Park, Arizona
articles.summary: Altitude Trampoline Park is expanding with a new venue in Litchfield Park, Arizona, continuing the brand's growth in the competitive trampoline park segment. The opening adds another branded trampoline park to the Phoenix metro market, increasing competitive dynamics for existing indoor entertainment operators in the region. Independent FEC operators in the area should evaluate their programming and pricing in response to this new entry.

articles.title: Sky Zone Trampoline Park Opens New Location, Adding Jobs and Family Entertainment
articles.summary: Sky Zone is opening a new trampoline park location, bringing family entertainment options and employment opportunities to a new community. The expansion reflects the ongoing growth trajectory of the trampoline park sector as a staple FEC offering. Operators and suppliers in the active entertainment space should note Sky Zone's continued aggressive expansion strategy.

articles.title: Spare Time Texas Positions Affordable Indoor Entertainment as a Key Value Proposition
articles.summary: Spare Time Texas in Pflugerville is being highlighted for offering affordable indoor entertainment options, including bowling, arcade games, and food and beverage, appealing to cost-conscious families. The coverage underscores how value positioning is becoming an important differentiator for FECs competing against higher-priced alternatives. Operators should consider how their pricing and bundling strategies communicate value to budget-aware guests.

articles.title: Lefty's Alley and Eats Opens New Bowling and Dining Venue in Newark, Delaware
articles.summary: Lefty's Alley and Eats has opened a new location in Newark, Delaware, combining bowling lanes with a full food and beverage program in a format that reflects the continued popularity of eatertainment concepts. The venue adds to the growing number of hybrid dining-and-entertainment destinations competing with traditional FECs. Operators in the eatertainment space can use this opening to benchmark their own food, beverage, and experience mix.

articles.title: Eternity Bowling to Showcase EV99 String Bowling System at Fun Asia Expo 2026
articles.summary: Eternity Bowling will display its EV99 string bowling system at the Fun Asia Expo 2026 in Indonesia, highlighting growing interest in string pin technology as an alternative to traditional pinsetter systems in FEC bowling installations. String bowling systems are gaining traction among operators for their lower maintenance costs and reliability in high-traffic environments. Bowling center operators and FEC developers considering new or replacement lanes should evaluate this technology.

articles.title: GTI China Expo 2026 Expands to 100,000 Square Meters, Covering VR, Arcades, and More
articles.summary: The 18th GTI China Expo in Guangzhou has expanded its exhibition area to over 100,000 square meters, up from 80,000 in 2025, and will cover more than 60 industry segments including arcade games, VR/AR, water park equipment, and children's entertainment. The event runs September 10-12 and serves as a major global procurement platform for amusement and entertainment suppliers. FEC operators and buyers sourcing new equipment or technology should consider attending or monitoring announcements from the expo.

articles.title: InfoComm 2026: AV Industry Prioritizes Ease of Operation Over Raw Performance
articles.summary: InfoComm 2026 revealed a notable industry shift toward making complex audiovisual systems simpler to deploy, operate, and maintain, even as raw performance metrics like lumens and resolution continue to advance. For FEC operators running projection-based attractions, immersive experiences, or large-format displays, this trend toward user-friendly AV systems could reduce staffing complexity and maintenance costs. The shift is relevant to any venue investing in new media-driven entertainment installations.

articles.title: Hockey Hall of Fame Upgrades Theater Projection with Christie Systems
articles.summary: The Hockey Hall of Fame has installed new Christie projectors in its TSN Theatre and Tim Horton's Theatre, replacing end-of-life equipment to maintain high-quality visitor experiences in its entertainment venues. The project demonstrates the importance of proactive AV equipment lifecycle planning for attraction operators. FEC and venue operators running theater or large-format projection experiences should note the upgrade timeline and vendor selection process as a planning reference.

articles.title: Museum of Ice Cream Las Vegas Flagship Demonstrates Experiential Entertainment's Broad Appeal
articles.summary: A detailed review of the Museum of Ice Cream's new Las Vegas flagship at Area15 examines how the immersive, photo-driven venue appeals across age groups, including skeptical teenagers and experience-seeking adults, by removing the typical generational divide in family entertainment. The analysis is useful for FEC operators thinking about how to design experiences with universal appeal rather than targeting a single demographic. It also reinforces the commercial viability of novelty-driven, shareable experience concepts.

articles.title: Parky's FEC at Moorestown Mall Back on Track After Lawsuit Resolution
articles.summary: Parky's, a family entertainment center planned for Moorestown Mall in New Jersey, is moving forward again after a legal dispute was resolved, clearing the path for the venue to proceed with its development. The case illustrates the legal and operational risks that can delay FEC openings in mall-based locations. Developers and operators pursuing mall anchor or inline FEC projects should ensure robust contract protections and dispute resolution mechanisms are in place.

articles.title: Fiesta Village FEC in Colton, California Closes After Decades of Operation
articles.summary: Fiesta Village, a long-running family entertainment center in Colton, California, has permanently closed, drawing crowds for a final farewell. The closure is a reminder of the competitive and financial pressures facing legacy FEC properties that have not modernized their attraction mix or guest experience. Operators in mature markets should consider this as a case study in the importance of continuous reinvestment and evolution.

articles.title: Developer of Proposed $3B Romulus Sports and Entertainment Complex Charged with Fraud
articles.summary: The developer behind a proposed $3 billion sports and entertainment complex in Romulus, Michigan has been charged with fraud, casting serious doubt on the project's future. Large-scale mixed-use entertainment developments carry significant due diligence requirements, and this case highlights the risks for investors, municipalities, and anchor tenants involved in such projects. FEC operators and investors evaluating partnerships with major development projects should scrutinize developer track records and financing structures carefully.

articles.title: SEGA Opens Angry Birds-Themed Attraction in Riyadh to Revitalize the IP
articles.summary: SEGA has opened an Angry Birds-themed entertainment attraction in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia, as part of a broader strategy to monetize the once-dominant but now struggling gaming IP through physical entertainment experiences. The move reflects the ongoing trend of gaming brands seeking revenue through location-based entertainment as mobile game revenues plateau. FEC operators and attraction developers tracking IP licensing opportunities in the Middle East should monitor this concept's performance.

articles.title: Dippin' Dots Celebrates National Ice Cream Day with Free Giveaways at 128 Franchise Locations
articles.summary: Dippin' Dots is marking National Ice Cream Day on July 19 with free mini cup giveaways at all 128 franchise locations nationwide, along with a sweepstakes for a year of free product. As a staple concession item in many FECs, bowling centers, and amusement parks, Dippin' Dots promotions can drive foot traffic to host venues. Operators carrying Dippin' Dots should ensure they are participating in and promoting the campaign to capture incremental visits.

articles.title: Michigan's Adventure Offers Free Admission for Guests 70+ to Celebrate 70th Season
articles.summary: Michigan's Adventure is marking its 70th season by offering free admission to guests aged 70 and older on select weekdays through a program called the 70 Plus Club. The campaign is a creative loyalty and community engagement strategy that drives midweek attendance and generates positive press. FEC operators looking for low-cost promotional ideas to fill off-peak time slots can draw inspiration from this targeted, story-driven initiative.

articles.title: IAAPA Expo Europe 2026 Achieves Certified Sustainable Event Status
articles.summary: IAAPA Expo Europe 2026 has been certified as a Sustainable Event following an independent third-party review, scoring 76% against mandatory sustainability requirements. The certification signals that sustainability standards are becoming more prominent in the attractions industry's major trade events. FEC operators and suppliers attending the expo can expect sustainability-focused programming and may want to align their own practices with emerging industry benchmarks.

articles.title: Boxing Arcade Machines Emerge as a Popular Revenue Driver for Entertainment Venues
articles.summary: Boxing arcade machines are gaining traction in modern entertainment venues as a high-engagement, competitive attraction that appeals to a wide demographic including teens and young adults. The format offers strong revenue potential with relatively low floor space requirements, making it attractive for FEC operators looking to diversify their arcade mix. Operators evaluating new arcade equipment should consider the category's growing popularity and repeat-play potential.
